evidence: We have a dedicated team and governance structure in place, known as Enterprise Model Governance ('EMG'). Our EMG team oversees the development, deployment and monitoring of AI models.
Our AI Center of Enablement ('AI COE') expands on EMG and brings together individuals from across our technology, privacy, data governance, security, legal, compliance, marketing and other teams.
We use artificial intelligence to support health care transformation by helping to enable the next generation of accessible, effective, affordable and enhanced health care solutions.

AI models can facilitate personalized solutions for individuals, inform earlier interventions and simplify health care experiences. There is human engagement in claims reviews, including expert decisions made by physicians.

The combination of our predictive analytics, and our machine learning and deep learning capabilities help to uncover trends, anticipate needs and create actionable intelligence that assists health care professionals.
We are currently subject to litigation claiming that we improperly used AI in the claims evaluation process.

Generative AI has been known to produce false or hallucinatory inferences; can produce inaccurate, incomplete or misleading content; unintended biases; errors and inadequacies, any of which may not be easily detectable.

Our use of AI/ML technologies has resulted and could continue to result in additional compliance costs, regulatory investigations and actions, and lawsuits. We are currently subject to litigation claiming improper AI use in claims evaluation.
If we are unable to use AI/ML, or if regulators restrict our ability to use AI/ML for certain purposes, it could make our business less efficient, result in competitive disadvantages.

limitations: No quantified dollar amounts, headcount figures, or budget allocations tied to AI investment were found in the excerpts; investment intensity scored 3 (named programs, no quantified scale).

Narrative concreteness scored 4 based on named deployed use cases (claims evaluation AI, predictive analytics, ML/deep learning in production) with operational detail, but no quantified business results (e.g., cost savings %, volume handled) were disclosed in the excerpts.

Risk disclosure scored 4 due to named company-specific risk scenarios with mechanisms (litigation, regulatory restriction, hallucination risks, compliance costs), but no quantified risk exposure or formal named oversight body beyond EMG/AI COE governance descriptions.
